LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WINSTON LUM'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED: GRANTED. THE VOID DEFAULT IS VACATED BASED ON EQUITY AND CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 473(D). THE DEFAULT NEVER SHOULD HAVE BEEN ENTERED BECAUSE (1) PLAINTIFF NEVER HAD LEAVE TO MAKE NEW ALLEGATIONS AGAINST MR. LUM AND (2) MR. LUM'S ANSWER TO THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT WAS SUFFICIENT TO CONTEST THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT BECAUSE THE AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS NOT SUBSTANTIVELY DIFFERENT WITH REGARD TO MR. LUM. MR. LUM HAS LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED ANSWER IF HE WISHES TO DO SO BY JUNE 1, 2012. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

No other Appearance. The Tentative Ruling is adopted.
DEFENDANT WINSTON LUM'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is Granted. The void default is vacated based on equity and Code of Civil Procedure section 473(d). Off Calendar Jan-05-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT FEDEX OFFICE AND PRINT SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFF, COMMONWEALTH LAND TITLE INSURANCE COMPANY.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO ASSERT A CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST FEDEX, THE HEADING FOR EACH CAUSE OF ACTION SHOULD UNAMBIGUOUSLY SO STATE. (CRC 2.112.) DEMURRER OVERRULED AS TO 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION. IF THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALLEGED AGAINST FEDEX, PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT SHOW A DUTY TO SUPERVISE/OVERSEE WAS OWED, THE DUTY WAS BREACHED, HOW IT WAS BREACHED, AND THE BREACH WAS THE FACTUAL AND PROXIMATE CAUSE OF DAMAGES TO PLAINTIFF. THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION MUST SPECIFY WHICH ACTS OF LANGUBAN IT IS ASSERTING FEDEX HAS LIABILITY FOR. (Moving party to prepare a proposed form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
